title: rubus callosus var. viscosus
intro: Rubus callosus var. viscosus, also known as the climbing blackberry or sticky blackberry, is a variant of the Rubus callosus plant species, characterized by its sticky hairs and edible blackberries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The health benefits of Rubus callosus var. viscosus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
